title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored a game-high 36 points, leading the Celtics to a 109-102 victory over the Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points and 12 rebounds, playing 36 minutes despite struggling with his shooting consistency.
Jordan Walsh added defensive strength with three blocks, while the Celtics benefited from a controversial foul call late in the game.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after recovering from a ruptured Achilles’ tendon, motivated by the team's success.
Despite being below his usual shooting percentages, Tatum has excelled in rebounding, averaging 9.2 rebounds per game since his return.
The Celtics, currently in second place, aim for a strong playoff position with Tatum back, showing potential for a home playoff series.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was fatally shot in a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard Friday afternoon.
The shooting followed an altercation between two armed men who knew each other, according to officials.
The Middlesex DA's Office and local police are actively investigating the incident; no public threat remains.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will move to Houston for the 2027 season, bought by the Houston Rockets' Fertitta family.
The Sun's sale is reportedly for a WNBA-record $300 million, surpassing a previous $325 million deal attempt.
The team will be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the city's former disbanded WNBA team.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' stars Ana de Armas in a crime syndicate spinoff, streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice' is a Vince Vaughn-led time-travel action-comedy available on Hulu.
'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 featuring Charlie Cox and Krysten Ritter is now streaming on Disney+.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out with left Achilles tendinitis for the Celtics' game against the Atlanta Hawks.
Joe Mazzulla describes Brown's condition as 'day-to-day' after a recent 31-point game against the Oklahoma City Thunder.
Nikola Vucevic is also out with a right ring finger fracture; Derrick White and Neemias Queta are available for the game.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington are phasing out gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions restrictions.
A Boston.com poll shows 73% of respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality and impact on landscaping needs.
Supporters of the ban highlight issues like noise, fumes, and health concerns, while suggesting a shift to quieter alternatives.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 COVID variant, known as 'cicada', has been detected in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2, originating from South Africa in 2024, shows potential to evade antibodies from 2025-2026 vaccines.
The WHO reports BA.3.2 poses low additional risk, with no increase in severe cases or hospitalizations noted.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge allowed a lawsuit to proceed against two DCF employees for the removal of children in Waltham.
The lawsuit alleges DCF workers violated constitutional rights by seizing children without parental consent or a warrant.
The children were taken due to a hospital report but later returned; an investigation found no evidence of abuse.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food & Wine magazine lists three New England restaurants as top anticipated openings for spring 2026.
Willie's in Beacon Hill, led by Chef Jamie Bissonnette, offers pizzas and globally-influenced pastas.
Ladyfish pop-up in Portland opens May 1, combining Maine seafood staples with Chinese and Italian influences.
